title: Valley National Bancorp Declares its Regular Quarterly Preferred and Common Stock Dividends
author: Na
description: Valley National Bancorp (NASDAQ: VLY) announced its regular quarterly dividend declarations. The company will pay preferred stock dividends ranging from $0.481745 to $0.515625 per share across three series, and a common stock dividend of $0.11 per share, with all payments scheduled for late June and early July 2026. The common stock dividend remained unchanged from the previous quarter.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company maintained its common stock dividend at $0.11 per share with no increase from the previous quarter, indicating stable but not growing shareholder returns. While dividend maintenance is positive for income investors, the lack of growth and the company's own disclaimer that dividends should not be used as an indicator of future payments suggests a cautious outlook.

title: Valley National Bancorp Announces Pricing Of Subordinated Notes
author: Na
description: Valley National Bancorp announced the pricing of $500 million in 6.219% Fixed-to-Floating Rate Subordinated Notes due 2036. The notes will carry a fixed rate until June 2031, then convert to a floating rate based on Three-Month Term SOFR plus 243 basis points. Valley intends to use proceeds to redeem its 3.00% subordinated notes due 2031 and for general corporate purposes. The offering is expected to close on May 14, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is executing a routine debt refinancing strategy by issuing new subordinated notes at a higher rate (6.219%) to replace lower-rate debt (3.00%), which reflects rising interest rate environment but is a standard capital management activity. No material negative or positive business developments are indicated.

title: Valley National Bancorp Declares Its Regular Quarterly Preferred and Common Stock Dividends
author: Globe Newswire
description: Valley National Bancorp (NASDAQ: VLY) announced its regular quarterly dividend declarations for shareholders of record on March 13, 2026. The company declared preferred stock dividends ranging from $0.470324 to $0.515625 per share across three series, and maintained its common stock dividend at $0.11 per share, unchanged from the previous quarter.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company maintained its common stock dividend at the previous quarter's level ($0.11 per share) and declared preferred dividends, demonstrating financial stability and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. Regular dividend declarations indicate consistent profitability and shareholder-friendly capital allocation policies.

title: Green Thumb Industries Announces an Additional $50 Million Senior Debt Financing
author: Green Thumb Industries Inc.
description: Green Thumb Industries announced a $50 million increase to its syndicated credit facility with Valley National Bank, bringing the total to $189 million. The funds will be used for general corporate purposes, strategic investments, and working capital. The facility matures in September 2029 and carries interest at SOFR + 500 basis points.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Valley National Bank is mentioned as the lead bank in the syndication, showing continued confidence in Green Thumb's creditworthiness. However, this is a routine financing transaction with no material impact on the bank itself.

title: Why This $3.6 Million Bank Buy Looks Smart Amid a 35% Stock Run
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Leeward Investments purchased 322,500 shares of Valley National Bancorp (VLY) for approximately $3.58 million in Q4, increasing its position to $22.33 million. The stock has surged 35.5% over the past year, outperforming the S&P 500. Valley National closed 2025 with record quarterly earnings, improved net interest margins, strong deposit growth, and disciplined loan management, positioning it as a normalized profitability play rather than a recovery story.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company delivered record Q4 earnings with net income rising to $195.4 million, achieved a 12-basis-point sequential expansion in net interest margin to 3.17%, generated $1.0 billion in deposit growth including $500 million in non-interest-bearing deposits, and maintained disciplined loan growth. The stock has appreciated 35.5% over the past year, significantly outperforming the S&P 500's 14% gain, indicating strong market confidence and normalized profitability trajectory.
